hook: If you died tomorrow, would your family know what you wanted — or would they be left guessing?
body:
If you died tomorrow, would your family know what you wanted — or would they be left guessing? Without a will, the state decides who gets your home, your savings, and your belongings. It's a process that's slow, expensive, and completely out of your hands.

A will is the most basic piece of an estate plan, yet most people over 50 still don't have one — or have one that's years out of date. Life changes. Your plan should too.

If you're not sure where you stand, a quick assessment can show you exactly what's missing and what to do next. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Most families don't find out their parents had no plan until it's too late to do anything about it.
body:
Most families don't find out their parents had no plan until it's too late to do anything about it. When a parent passes without a will, the family is left scrambling — trying to locate accounts, figure out property ownership, and navigate probate court, all while grieving.

It's an uncomfortable conversation to start, but knowing whether your parents have a plan in place is one of the most practical things you can do for your whole family right now. You don't need to be pushy. You just need to know.

Will & Estate Ready can help you understand what a solid estate plan looks like — so you know the right questions to ask. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: If something happened to you today, who would raise your kids — and would a court agree with your choice?
body:
If something happened to you today, who would raise your kids — and would a court agree with your choice? Without a will that names a guardian, that decision doesn't belong to you. It belongs to a judge who doesn't know your family, your values, or what your children need.

Naming a guardian is one of the most important things a parent can do, and one of the most overlooked. It doesn't have to be complicated — it just has to be done.

If you're not sure whether your estate plan covers guardianship the way it should, find out now. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Probate is the legal process your family goes through when you die — and without a plan, it can take years.
body:
Probate is the legal process your family goes through when you die — and without a plan, it can take years. Court fees, attorney costs, and public records all come with it. Assets get frozen. Beneficiaries wait. And the outcome isn't always what you would have wanted.

The good news is that probate is largely avoidable with the right planning. A will, a trust, properly titled accounts — these are the tools that keep your estate out of court and your family out of limbo.

Not sure if your current plan would hold up? Find out where you stand before it matters. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: If your parent had a stroke tomorrow and couldn't speak for themselves, could you legally make decisions on their behalf?
body:
If your parent had a stroke tomorrow and couldn't speak for themselves, could you legally make decisions on their behalf? Without a power of attorney in place, the answer is almost certainly no — even if you're their child and their closest family member.

A durable power of attorney lets someone step in to manage finances, pay bills, and handle legal matters when a person can't do it themselves. Without one, families often have to go to court just to get that authority — and that process is slow, expensive, and emotionally draining.

Knowing whether your parents have this document is just as important as knowing whether they have a will. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: Life insurance pays out — but only if the right people are named, in the right amounts, with the right policy still active.
body:
Life insurance pays out — but only if the right people are named, in the right amounts, with the right policy still active. A lot of young parents assume they're covered because they have a policy through work. But group coverage through an employer often isn't enough, and it doesn't follow you if you leave your job.

Life insurance is only one piece of a family's financial safety net. It works best when it's connected to a clear estate plan — so the money actually gets to the people who need it, the way you intended.

Do you know if your coverage is enough? Start by understanding where your whole plan stands. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.

hook: A will tells people what you want — a trust makes sure it actually happens the way you intended.
body:
A will tells people what you want — a trust makes sure it actually happens the way you intended. Trusts aren't just for the ultra-wealthy. They're practical tools that let you control how and when your assets are distributed, keep your estate out of probate, and protect what you've built from unnecessary delays and costs.

With the ongoing conversation around taxes and retirement savings — Roth accounts, Social Security timing, capital gains — how your estate is structured matters more than most people realize. The decisions you make now affect what your family actually receives later.

If you're not sure whether a trust makes sense for your situation, a good first step is understanding where your overall plan stands. Get your estate readiness score at willestateready.com — $21, 10 minutes.
